title: US does not have capacity to unilaterally open Strait of Hormuz: expert | ABC NEWS
description: Jared Mondschein from the US Studies Centre says Iran is leveraging its asymmetric advantages to deny US primacy over the Strait of Hormuz. title: ADF to assist with flood clean-up in Katherine, as other communities remain submerged | ABC NEWS
description: The Federal Minister for Emergency Management has confirmed the Australian Defence Force will be assisting with the flood clean-up in the Katherine region with around 50 members of the force on the ground from Monday. Meanwhile other communities in the Northern Territory remain flooded, with homes in the Daly River region still under water and a weather system in Central Australia under close-watch. title: Fifth member of Iranian football team gives up asylum in Australia | ABC NEWS
description: A fifth member of the Iranian women’s national football team has withdrawn her bid for asylum in Australia and left the country. There are now only two left of the original seven members who were granted asylum by Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke after the team’s exit from the Asian Cup.  Members of the Iranian diaspora allege the Iranian official who sought asylum was relaying threats from the regime to the players who refused to travel out of the Australia with the squad. title: The truth behind the toxic algal bloom | ABC News Daily podcast
description: It was one of the worst environmental disasters to strike Australia’s oceans.    When a massive algal bloom arrived in South Australia, devastating the coastline and killing marine life, the public was initially told it wasn’t toxic, when it later became apparent it was.   Today, Four Corners reporter Angus Grigg on his investigation into what the South Australian government knew and whether it acted fast enough.   Featured:    Angus Grigg, Four Corners reporter

title: Three missing fishermen confirmed dead off South Australian coast | ABC NEWS
description: South Australia police have confirmed three men who were missing off the state’s south-eastern coast are dead.  Authorities were alerted after a car and trailer were left unattended on a boat ramp in Beachport on a day of rough waters and windy conditions. Their bodies have now been recovered as police urge people to note the coordinates of debris to assist in their investigation. title: US energy secretary confident international navies can open Strait of Hormuz | ABC NEWS
description: US Energy Secretary Chris Wright says he is confident foreign powers, including China and the UK, will join US efforts to secure the Strait of Hormuz.  The Trump official said he believed the war in the Middle East could end in weeks but stopped short of confirming whether petrol prices would drop.  The UK is considering whether to get involved in the US operation by sending its mine-hunting drones. title: Adoptees want government inquiry into South Korean adoption program expanded | ABC NEWS
description: The federal government is being urged to broaden a new inquiry into a South Korean adoption program found to have a history of corruption and fraud. Behind the push are numerous inter-country adoptees who arrived in Australia from the 1970s onwards as international adoption grew in popularity. But many later learned stories about their biological parents were false or parts of their adoption paperwork had been fabricated. title: King and Queen of Denmark greet Australian public in Canberra | ABC NEWS
description: King Frederik and the Australian-born Queen Mary of Denmark had a meet-and-greet with the Australian public in Canberra on the third day of their royal visit to Australia.  The tour is the royal couple’s first state visit since the Danish monarch's coronation.  The pair attended a state banquet hosted by Governor-General Sam Mostyn and Prime Minister Anthon Albanese the previous night.
